Then a bit of a lengthy chat with Emma about life and how she's generally a bit down and stuff, which was a bit of an eye opener, and kinda stressed me out a bit. I think she's mostly just down about her job and stuff, and that I could be doing more better stuff, less to disappoint her. I think we polished over it, I explained a bit of my new plan (what I know of it so far) and how I'm gonna try and work out my direction and that. I've been reading "frictionless freelancing" and its been pretty good so far. Deffo rocking a new level of motivation, at the very least. Think it'll be motivation mixed in with a few pointers for me, it's mostly geared at designers/writers (which I might end up dabbling in anyway), but there's some stuff that sort of cross pollinates into music. Stoked I finally signed up and joined the MU the other day too. Time to start getting some results I think. But I'm only get that from definite, positive, actions. And more than one/sustained/repeated ones. Think my housework plan will help a little at least, so at least she'll be happier when she is home, and there'll be less/minimal "days off" where all she does is clean (or at least it takes up a good chunk of the day). That'll help, I gotta try and remember that it's nice to see Emma playing with the kids, enjoying them for what they are rather than getting annoyed because of what they have/haven't done. 

Might work out when I started keeping this diary, then work out a good interval to go back and read the ends from a while back, like three months, six months, or something similar.

Direction would be good, so I can always ask "is this helping me get to where I want to be". Or , I spose, "is this helping me get to *who* I want to be?"

425 words. I reckon I could easily spout that into somewhere more public once a week or so. Gotta get in it.

Me thinking that's pretty cool makes me feel really old.
Gonna aim for a haircut tomorrow morning. Now I shall sort the kitchen out a bit, have a play of my bass for ten minutes, rough up a plan for tomorrow, then get the hell to bed I reckon.

## [How to change a lightbulb](http://notacello.co.uk/blog/2013/4/15/how-to-change-a-lightbulb.html)

Just re-watched [this][1]. 

> It's easier to do trivial things that are urgent, than it is to do important things that are not urgent (like thinking), and it's also easier to do little things we know we can do, than to start big things we're not so sure about. 

I think I'd forgotten just how much _great stuff_ there is in here. I think it's another one of those things that needs to be read, listened to, etc every now and then, and each time you get something slightly different out of it.

   [1]: http://vimeo.com/18913413

#social #blogging

#thingskidssay


	Date:	18 April 2013 at 19:18:13 BST
	Weather:	12°C Mostly Sunny
	Location:	17 Townsend, Banbury, England, United Kingdom

Think I've given up with my flirtation with Taskpaper. I can see how it's really good/useful for some things, but the power of Omnifocus is just a little too great. If I wasn't spoiled by that, I think it'd work great. One of the major drawbacks is the lack of repeating tasks. It's so easy to bung something in there and have it show up every week (or whatever), and Taskpaper just can't do that.i spose I could put them on the calendar instead, but there are some things that don't have a specific time (or even day) that just need a little prompting to happen between say Tuesday morning and Wednesday night, that having them as part of the "hard landscape" wouldn't make sense, and would probably lead to me relaxing slightly about the validity of anything on the calendar, which'd mean actual hard landscape things would slip.
Taskpaper is going to be great for shopping list type things, or listing small tasks that need to get done before something else happens ("get bag ready for gig") that Omnifocus can be a little cumbersome for, or just because I don't want "important" things getting mixed in with "buy tomato sauce" or "download new walking dead". So using drafts to send grocery type items to a shopping list on Dropbox that Taskpaper looks at is a pretty good, fast solution that uses less taps and fiddling (no project or context selection) than adding the same thing to Omnifocus.
The power of Taskpaper and even moreso foldingtext is *very* interesting though, pretty sure I'll find more and more uses for them, especially if an iOS version of foldingtext was to appear. I think that's slightly holding me back from ditching byword just yet
That was a dead fucking easy 300 words to write. I'm pretty sure I could clean it up a bit, add some links, elaborate on stuff and turn it into a blog post of some sort

	Date:	18 April 2013 at 23:40:45 BST
	Weather:	9°C Mostly Clear

	Date:	11 May 2013 at 08:15:28 BST
	Weather:	8°C Showers Nearby
	Location:	2 Townsend, Banbury, England, United Kingdom

So it's early Saturday morning, I've been awake for a few minutes (long enough to do a round on the 4ish sites I habitually check) and now I'm going to do some writing. That Stephen king book has a lot to answer for. I doubt there's any way I'll churn out *ten pages* every day. Or even every week, but I reckon I can do a decent chunk. I might go back and look at my "apps" list that I sent to Tess, and rework that into a blog post, or several smaller ones.
I don't think I'll be much of a fiction writer either, but ya never know, maybe one day. For new though, I mainly want to write stuff that is going to add some sort of value. Not sure what to, myself? I think it'd be nice to have my stuff on the Internet, so that anyone who stumbles across it can read it and have seem idea of who I am, or how I do stuff. I'm not sure I want it to get particularly personal, maybe on the odd post, but mostly I want it to enhance my *brand* whatever the fuck that means.
So nerdy stuff, computery stuff, but I guess most importantly, musical stuff. It'd help if I did more musical stuff, but perhaps one will drive the other. I've definitely got the hankering to play music again in the last day or so. Like if I want to be any good, I need to work that muscle. And I definitely want to be half decent. I'm not thinking *adam Ben Ezra* good, but I think I have quite a bit of catching up to do just to get to the level of good psycho/rockabilly/punk player really. I think I'll spend the next couple of weeks learning (or relearning) other people's songs. That should be fun, teach me some things about how songs are put together, make me play things in ways I perhaps wouldn't have played them, and it's always pretty cool when you go to see a band and hear a riff by another band you like. It'll give me a bunch of options when I'm sorting out some sort of jam night or more "local" band after the move too. Get some common ground. I'll be *better*.
I really need to get some progress on the house too. Pretty much just treading water with keeping it reasonably tidy, which isn't enough. I think I'll try and imagine that we could move tomorrow, and try and get the house ready for that situation. So as much as possible we could just grab our stuff and leg it. I guess I could do my stuff first. Anything that I don't need can get boxed/bagged up (geek room, I'm looking at you) and then start on the rest of the house.
Today though, I need to get up, have a bath, get the kids dressed, go to aldi, come back, cut the grass out the front at least, and get a bunch of the stuff deemed "important" in Omnifocus ticked the hell off. There's no way I expected to get to Saturday and there still be 20+ things in there. I do have until Tuesday though. Tuesday is review day from now on.
Seems I can hit 500 words pretty easily, without any planning or structure or anything really. I spose I've covered quite a range of topics in not much detail.
Oh yea, I also wanted to see how long I could go without my laptop for. It doesnt get much use other than for sharing things properly on Facebook, watching movies and downloading torrents. Then again, I really wanna have a good go with scapple and get some broader planning done. I think the planning will help me get motivated and get some movement on a lot of the stuff I've been slacking on. Like has been said, my brain knows there's *something* I have to be doing, but because it can't work out what that is, it lets me sit around, scanning the same few websites and Facebook over and over and over and over. And over. Gotta snap out of this routine

	Date:	11 May 2013 at 23:50:05 BST

	Date:	20 May 2013 at 16:49:40 BST
	Weather:	16°C Cloudy
	Location:	3–7 Springfield Avenue, Banbury, England, United Kingdom

Everyone at work was freaking out over some proposed (read: happening soon) changes to timetables and day start/end times. I kinda understand why, finishing later would suck for anyone that has kids, for one thing. Probably not going to kick in this year though, so I'm not that worried. Very mercenary!
Was envigilating an exam this arvo, and apart from spending a bunch of time remembering how to play "this charming man", I realised that ever since I was at/finished school I've been saying something along the lines of "I would've done alright if it wasn't for...."  or "not too bad considering......"
Which is pretty poor. It was sparked by thinking about GCSE science, and how I managed a B grade, despite my coursework being either shit or non-existent. Or both. And this pattern has continued. I'm very much reminded of something I read/heard about fear of failure and procrastination. Like if I'm not invested in something properly, and leave it til the last minute, or just don't *try* that hard, then it doesn't matter if the end result isn't good.
I don't think I want that to be the way my life plays out. "Not too bad, considering" is no legacy. This might become a blog post

## [Nerd Alert](http://notacello.co.uk/blog/2013/5/28/nerd-alert-1.html)

This was originally an email to someone fantastic who just got an iPad, so it's mostly iOS apps with a hint of OS X. I figured there were a few good things in here that might be of interest to someone else, so I gone and stuck it up here with a little tweaking, enjoy (or don't): 

## Apps

### [Omnifocus][1]:

Pretty nerdy but powerful task management thing. Takes a bit of setting up, but pretty awesome once it's running. It's pretty versatile too, there are many different ways to have it do things, I wouldn't take any _one way_ as **_the_** way to have it set up. Get it going, have a look at the way other people use it, and steal some ideas from all of them, or something. There's definitely a danger of fiddling with this stuff instead of doing any actual work though, so it's good to be aware of that, and save fiddling for certain (not busy) times.   
Some [vids][2] might give you some idea if it's for you, [More nerdy][3] stuff that might risk going over your head if you're a newby. The Mac app has a free trial, so that could be worth a try, at least. Theres a pretty good [book][4] too.   
[This][5] is the first episode in a series of podcasts with the dreaded [talking mann][6] that's kinda relevant to omnifocus too. Episodes [97][7], [98][8], [99][9] are the others.   
[Things][10] is meant to be good too, but I haven't used it.

### [Taskpaper][11]

Is great too, and a lot simpler (and cheaper) than the other apps. No repeating tasks, or timers, or alerts but there's something about the clean-ness and _simplicity_ that's appealing to me. At the moment I mainly use it for simpler lists, like for shopping, more mindless stuff that needs less management and more just getting done and ticking off. I'm very, _very_ tempted to switch to it in place of omnifocus but there are a few hurdles to overcome, and I'm not sure it's worth the extra fiddling. For me. Right now.

[This][12] is a pretty good post kinda evaluating the different apps, and [this guy][13] kinda goes into way too much detail on why some stuff might want to "live" in a different place to other stuff.

### [Day One][14]

Awesome Journal app. Syncs over Dropbox _or_ iCloud so you can write on mac or iPad (or iPhone) and it shows up everywhere. I never really kept a diary before, but it's pretty handy, and I write in some version of this nearly every day. Habit is good. [Here's][15] a bunch of use cases/ideas too.

### [Goodreader][16]

Not sure how relevant this is any more, but it used to be pretty must have. Goodreader will open pretty much any type of file with text in it, and even do annotations to PDFs and stuff. I still have it installed, but haven't opened it in a loooooong time.

### [Flipboard][17]

Not quite got my head around this yet, but it does look very nice, and might be handy for when I just want something to read without having to think too hard about what it actually is that I want to read. Good for news, current events etc. 

### [Cheat sheet][18]

Shows you all the keyboard shortcuts for whatever app you're in when you hold down the ⌘ key. _Very_ handy. And can't be beaten on price.

### [Paper][19]

Briiiiiiiliant app for drawing/painting on the iPad. Love it. It's free, so go get it, then splash out on extra brushes and gadgets if you feel the urge.

### [Penultimate][20]

This is a bit better for scrawling stuff you might intend to _read_ on the iPad. Sometimes it's nice to do some almost old fashioned style writing. But on some expensive glass.

### Hippy

Been trying to use [calm.com][21] to chill out a bit and get in on the whole _mindfulness_ racket. Again, tis free for a while, so give it a go if it's interesting. 

### [Instapaper][22]

Awesome for saving stuff to read later. Great iPad/phone app, or you can use their website on the computer to look at everything you've "saved" too. I fire stuff into here pretty much 24/7, then go through it when I have time to read properly, which is usually early in the morning or after the kids are in bed.

## Writing

### Markdown

99.9999% of everything I write is in [markdown][23] now. Sometimes even on paper :/ Again, there's a really good [book][24] you should read on your iPad, and a [bunch][25] of [stuff][26] on the [internet][27] in general. Most of the apps I mentioned already will export markdown to .doc, .rtf, .pdf, .html etc, but are good for writing in anyway. Especially on the iPad, even if you just use it to get text into Dropbox and then have to cut n paste it into word for work or whatever (you poor souls). 

I've messed around with a few things for writing that are nice and quick, without the bloat of something like Pages or Word. [iA Writer][28] and [Writeroom][29] are pretty nice, I favour [Byword][30] though. No major reason why I don't think, just _feels_ better, and the pretty instantaneuous sync (via iCloud _or_ Dropbox) between the Mac, iPad and iPhone versions means I've always got access to whatever I've got brewing. This post definitely started off in Byword, but I'm finishing it off in the young whippersnapper that is [FoldingText][31]. FoldingText is almost like magic, and I like it a lot. I'd be on it like white on rice if there was an iOS version. I may have to look at sorting out folders and things so I can seemlessly use FT on the actual computer and Byword on the portable devices. 

### [nvALT][32]

Is an amazing little Mac app by [Mr Brett Terpstra][33]. I use it all the time on the mac for random bits of info, song ideas, notes, the start of something longer to write, serial numbers, reference numbers (stuff that I'll need **NOW** but probably not for long or very often), wifi passwords for places I visit, door security codes, etc. It's just super fast to search for some piece of info that's already in there or to add to something or start something new. **_Speedy_**. I've got it set up to sync everything to Dropbox, and I have Writeroom on the iPad/iPhone set up to look at that folder too for on the go access. That's the only thing I use Writeroom for, so Writeroom on the iPad is the "place" I go to for those quick notes/snippets, and Byword (on any device) is for anything longer, [blog][34]/[band][35] site posts, assignments when I was at college, cover letters for jobs, etc.

### Comics

I tend to use [Comicflow][36]. [Comixology][37] is good too but could get expensive fast!

## Music

[Garageband][38] is pretty hard to avoid. It's fun and pretty easy to knock an entire song up in minutes, with or without actual instruments. Obviously (hopefully) they can be copied over to Mr Mac for more fun if you feel so inclined. [Synth][39] is pretty great too. It makes some nice noises, and can pipe them into (or be used as a midi controller for) Garageband/Logic on the mac, as can [Pianist][40]. [Tunein][41] is pretty good for listening to the "radio" too, but for the more talky stuff, [Downcast][42] kicks the arse of the default podcasts app in so many ways it's a no-brainer.

### ADN

I'm pretty new to [App.net][43] but it seems pretty good, and is breaking away slightly from that "twitter clone" label it seemed to be stuck with for a while. Seems like decent folks on there, and a bit more of a _community_. I figure the old thing about _"The bigger the crowd gets, the dumber the crowd gets"_ is true, and why I find disgracebook and twitter less and less appealing. Just seems like a better signal/noise ratio. The platform as a whole is pretty interesting too, there's a lot going on in the background. That out of the way, on my iPhone I use [Riposte][44] which doesn't have an iPad version at the moment, so on the bigger screen I'm on [Netbot][45]. Both get the job done admirably, Riposte is just feels nicer.

Which kinda sums up the allegedly "useful" apps I open semi regularly. Now here's (unfortunately fake) Bruce Lee playing ping pong:

http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qHe6vhexm6g

	Date:	9 June 2013 at 21:42:43 BST

## [The king is dead.............](http://notacello.co.uk/blog/2013/6/9/the-king-is-dead.html)

It's strange how spending _years_ listening to someone's songs seems like you're getting to know them. Without ever having talked to Torr, I can still kinda tell that he was one of the good guys. I guess he talked to me, without ever knowing it. Sadly, it'd [appear he never will][1]. Torr Skoog has left the building.  
As frontman of the flat out _amazing_ [Kings of Nuthin][2] he said things that came from a totally different place from where I'm at, but that 99% of the time I could totally identify with. [Time passing us by][3] (and my lack of doing anything of much consequence with it), [the "joy" of school][4], [not getting on with the lady][5], [crappy jobs][6], [being][7] in [bands][8], [and][9] so [much][10] [more][11]. From the moment I heard these guys I've wanted a brass section. They come out looking like a classy swing band or something and then just annihilate you with punk rock. I think the difficulty for them to really fit into any sort of genre hampered them reaching a wider audience, but the lucky ones of us that knew them just lost someone important.  
Just so gutted, thoughts and condolences to the man's friends, family, bandmates, etc. It's never good when someone has died, we never get enough time with the ones that count.  
Raise a glass.  


   [1]: http://www.verbicidemagazine.com/2013/06/09/torr-skoog-kings-of-nuthin-dead/
   [2]: http://www.sailorsgraverecords.com/artist.php?id=thekingsofnuthin
   [3]: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vWiF8pWEQKw
   [4]: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WuKlmxPWuB0
   [5]: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5sUj9JZCKtc
   [6]: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nbDyx7SlKcQ
   [7]: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iMEnbqmEaeU
   [8]: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EdIxD4TOD3w
   [9]: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=N1rhgXsU2yw
   [10]: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dqkaTmOm2Dw
   [11]: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zkE2hkNtWF8

	Date:	24 June 2013 at 11:34:48 BST

Gig on saturday was pretty awesome. We played pretty well, the setup was great, the crowd seemed to like us, including the guy that looked an awful lot like [Rick Wakeman](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rick_Wakeman). Bit strange that Luke May had organised the whole thing, after we'd been in that ill-fated band :/
The other bands were alright, Red Crow were apparently inspired by us after we played the wheat sheaf in banners a while back, and kinda reminded me a little of *Larry and his flask*. 	Date:	12 July 2013 at 09:55:25 BST

## [So sick of not trying](http://notacello.co.uk/blog/2013/7/12/so-sick-of-not-trying.html)

I was invigilating an exam recently, and apart from spending a bunch of time remembering how to play [This Charming Man][1], I realised that ever since I was at/finished school I've been saying something along the lines of "I would've done alright if it wasn't for...." or "not too bad considering......" Which is pretty poor. It was sparked by thinking about GCSE science, and how I managed a couple of B grades (that's almost an A!), despite my coursework being either shit or non-existent. Or both. And this pattern has continued. I'm very much reminded of something I read/[heard][2] about fear of failure and procrastination. Like if I'm not invested in something properly, and leave it til the last minute, or just don't _try_ that hard, then I can tell myself it doesn't matter if the end result isn't great. I don't think I want that to be the way my life plays out. "Not too bad, considering" is no legacy. 

   [1]: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kGnjrTkv1gs
   [2]: http://5by5.tv/b2w

#social #blogging


	Date:	12 July 2013 at 23:50:10 BST

	Date:	23 August 2013 at 08:59:36 BST
	Weather:	16°C Sunny
	Location:	West Hill Gardens, Portishead, England, United Kingdom

Just had a really weird dream! I was driving my uncle (but I think he was James caan) who may have been a mob boss, to hospital, because he'd been shot. Like a lot. He was talking and stuff a bit at the start, but got worse and was just kinda sat in a wheelchair by the end. For some reason we were in some version of Banbury. We drove *through* the shopping centre (which was much bigger and involved some big sets of stairs), very carefully mind, and some security goon did sort of fumblingly say something about having to detain me until the police arrive, but was pretty happy to let me go when I explained the situation and said that I would be happy to come back once I'd sorted my uncle out. Then for some reason we weren't in the car anymore, and I was pushing him around in a chair. Up and down some more outside steps this time, and finally into the hospital! Which was all white and glass and super clean and quiet and looked a bit like something from minority report or demolition man or something. Sterile. No one waiting around, just very high ceilings and a desk with a couple of posh ladies behind it. I asked them which way to a+e, and they calmly informed me "sorry, love, we *dont* deal with them here anymore." Which freaked me out pretty good. Think I got a little ranty and was like "what the fuck do you suggest then!? There are doctors here though, right, and equipment, and etc? Get a doctor to look at him, and I'm sure he'll figure the rest out on his own!"
"Can't really do that"
"Well, can you get an ambulance here to take us to Oxford then?"
"Ooh no, I wouldn't mobilise him" (woman has a northern accent in my head now, big, but not super fat,just like a solid brick of a lady. Shortish but big in top gray with flecks of darker hair. Like a box head cut. Square hair for a square lady!)
"Just give us a minute and we'll see what we can do"
"What, so I should just leave him there in the corner to die and then you'll do something about it? He's come in here with more holes in him than a pack of polos and you just want us to "sit tight"?"
"Well I wouldn't go quite that fa"
At this point I really lose it (I think I might be Alec Baldwin) and start running all over the place, screaming for a doctor. We do find a waiting room, and I proceed to freak them the fuck out by screaming that they're all waiting to see a doctor, while we've been here 20 minutes (or whatever) leaving a nice trail of human juice wherever we go and no one is even willing to show us how to get to a doctor).
There's like a balcony thing all around the edge of the waiting room (looking down on it) and a bunch of doctor looking people are looking down on the scene, some a little shocked, some just doing that thing where they nudge people to keep moving and pretend they haven seen anything.
Think I woke up about there.
Oh, at some point while talking to the receptionists he tried to get up out of the wheelchair, but didn't put up much resistance when I told him to sit back down.

So many questions!

	Date:	20 September 2013 at 17:15:25 BST
	Weather:	14°C Partly Cloudy
	Location:	Harbour Road, Portishead, England, United Kingdom

Finally did my application to high down today. The form sucked balls bigstyle, like nowhere to express myself really apart from one section, et voila:


> Apart from having two young children of my own, I feel that during my time at Banbury Academy I learned a lot that could be applied to working with younger students. It would be great to "catch them early" to help them develop good habits and attitudes towards learning, in the hope that it will open up more options to them in the future and enable them to make better choices.
I think that my range of experience, musical background and interest in computers, games and gadgets is a great asset in finding some common ground with the students that can be used to get through to them, or to find real world examples/applications for the topic being taught which can grab or hold their interest.
A big part of my work at Banbury Academy was with a select few students as part of a successful team in the Ace Base. This was ran as an almost entirely separate school for children that were struggling severely in mainstream education. Our mission was to prepare them academically (often filling in large gaps that'd been missed in their studies) as well as to provide some life skills to help them become competent adults through budgeting, work experience, goal setting and (importantly) reviewing, perseverance, making mistakes, and finding ways to tie their interests into their lessons, or vice-versa. Volunteering at a Banbury primary school made me realise that I'd love the opportunity to instil some of those ideas into younger children so they could follow a better path.      




Which I don't think is too shabby




	Date:	20 September 2013 at 23:50:22 BST

## [-](http://notacello.co.uk/blog/2013/9/23/microsoft-surface.html)

## [Microsoft Surface mk2][1]

See, I think the problem the Surface has to some extent is Windows 8. It's a bit of a change, and most people haven't really used it, and a lot of the ones that have used it are put off by the redesign. With the iPad, Apple already had a pretty big customer base with iPhones (or iPod touches), so the with iPad being "just a big iPhone", a lot of people felt like they already knew how to use it et voilà, they sell millions of em.

   [1]: http://bgr.com/2013/09/23/microsoft-surface-2-hands-on-surface-pro-2-preview/

Gonna log my thoughts/homework for the *beyond the to do list* book in here.
## Day one (geddit?):
### What I'm doing now, to help work out what season of my life I'm in.
- Dad- kids are in school so I'm mostly free for those hours in the middle of the day. But they do have a lot of extra curricular stuff going on (swimming, drama, karate)
- husband- don't get to see Emma that much, and her work kinda sucks for my availability on weekends. That said, she can be pretty flexible if she knows in advance. I need to do a bit more to keep the house in a reasonable state.
- music- think doctor caligari could be a really good opportunity. Seem like they're on the rise (possibly a little stalled) higher than any previous band, and seem like jonny at least is a decent guy.
- production- very feeling the urge to get some music written, recorded, produced etc. I want to use those skills, could use some better gear though. Will use whatever I get from Banbury academy on that stuff.
- website- want to finish moving it over to ss6 and get posting on there more regularly. Twice a week
- tshirts- kinda stalled this. Might have to give it a bit of a kick, as it is a way I can start making money almost immediately, and things are getting a little tight.

	Date:	4 October 2013 at 10:54:58 BST
	Location:	Broadmead Bristol, Bristol, England, United Kingdom

Killing time in cafe amore before I go in some free course via the MU/feu. Reading more of steven king's *on writing*. Damn good read! Kinda got me motivated again to start writing some songs. Like just do it, and you'll learn how to do it. So might write a song or two, then out them away for six weeks and then revisit them. Treat them like he does novels. Then they'll be fresh, and not "my darlings" quite as much.

This doctor caligari thing seems to be going south. Now I'm not joining an already existing band, I'm helping reform it. Johnny is the only original member (although it appears there never was much of an actual band), and now we have some kid/girl singing, and the drummer is a bass player and stuff. It's becoming less what I want to do all the time. Like joining a band would be fine, great even, just learn the parts and turn up. Or forming something new from scratch (with me in charge) would also be cool (need to get some songs first!), but this is kinda in between and doesn't seem to have many of the benefits. I reckon I'll give it a go and at least get the Halloween gig out of the way, but I'm not sure this is a long term thing.

Should probably get going now, gonna lee then head over to the old Vic. Reckon I'll be well early but that's kinda what I want!

	Date:	4 October 2013 at 11:27:41 BST
	Weather:	15°C Drizzle
	Location:	6 King Street, Bristol, England, United Kingdom

Here now, drinking more coffee, waiting for things to start. Should be interesting. Some old dude in a laptop looks like he could be "my people". Wonder if he's here for the course too, and if we'll get to chat.

	Date:	4 October 2013 at 17:49:22 BST
	Weather:	15°C Mostly Cloudy
	Location:	College Green, Bristol, England, United Kingdom

Did find out that we're scuppered on the housing benefit front though, which is pretty annoying after all the fucking hoops they made us jump through. Does emphasise the importance if me sorting emerging out though, even more so than it had before.
Time for some clean ones and some separation and some motherfucking *focus*!!!

So, wheel of life via #goals again.
Career gets a 3. New band is alright, but they'll never be as into it as I am by the looks of things so far. I'm not really doing much else, and Emma has convinced me to sign on, which I can kind of agree with, but man I want to get my own thing going.
Social gets 5. Still not really seeing many people, Stu and that Roger guy seem pretty cool though, just need to nurture things a little I reckon. Again, I think being able to drive and get out in Bristol a bit more would help that, make some new contacts and stuff.
Family gets 7. Things could be better, but generally pretty happy.
Intellect gets 6. Like I know I'm not dumb, but I'm definitely not pushing myself at all lately. Might have a look at the open uni again, at least get a taster course or something done. Omnifocusing that now!
Physically and spiritually I'm not too bad. Sometimes get round to meditating, I've squeezed in a little exercise here and there. Could probably eat better, and once I get over this cold I think I'd like to ween myself off the caffeine a fair bit. Was thinking the other day that I probably have, as a minimum, one cup of tea every two hours, and often much more than that, so if I'm up from 7am-11pm (which is optimistic), that's 8 cups a day. Minimum. And not including the occasional coffee or coke if it's available.

Was listening to systematic last night with Merlin and Brett, and their ADD talk got me thinking that it's highly likely that's something I have, so I'd like to learn more about that, and myself, and deal with it a bit better.

Oh, and financially we're fucked, as is. Like could be worse, but we worked out we have £18 a week to spare, and we already blew next weeks', and possibly the week after, and it's gonna be really hard to stick to that, and we shouldn't fucking *have* to.

	Date:	13 October 2013 at 13:55:46 BST

Good for the band, better for me. That confidence of not having to worry so much if things are gonna sound shit. Just hope I haven't over tightened that pickup and damaged something. Get the blender set up properly, read up on gain staging so I'm doing that properly once it's going through the eq pedal and DI. Like just spend a bunch of time working out what works well with *my bass*. It's a bit shit that I don't even know that really.

	Date:	6 November 2013 at 21:20:38 GMT
	Weather:	13°C Rain
	Location:	87–95 West Hill, Portishead, England, United Kingdom

Timer is on!
Today was a bit of a washout, but not entirely unsuccessful. Started off pretty well anyway! Was struggling to get Emma to wake up, so I tried moving my (morning glorious) cock towards her face. To my pleasant surprise, she seemed quite pleased about this and slobbered all over it until it slobbered all over her (and me)!
So definitely off to a good start. 
Then got the kids off to school on time without much of an incident. Despite going in and out of the house a couple of times I still managed to leave the bike lock on the shelf, which was a little disappointing, but no real biggie. Did cause me to reschedule my fireworks ticket buying plans though. I’d originally planned to pick them up on the way back from dropping the kids off, but I didn’t much fancy it while carrying Beans’ bike, so did it on the way home after school instead, which worked out pretty well, she enjoyed the trip to the cake shop! Come to think of it, they probably weren’t even open that early.
Listened to the latest *We’re Alive* on the way home, which finished just in time after I got in, emptied/hung/reloaded the washing machine and made a brew. Then I sat down to watch the latest *Walking Dead* (S03E04) but quickly realised that where I left off was in fact last week’s episode, which I guess I missed because last week was busy and Emma was off (although I think she was working Monday, must’ve just forgotten). So quickly got hold of that episode (while more pottering around the house, and possibly the internet) and sat down for a bit of a marathon session of watching both. Was a bit wonky, but pretty damn good. I liked that they managed a minor homage to Tyrese in the comics, by having him seemingly doomed under a mass of walkers with nothing but a hammer, only for him to show up again a few minutes later, good stuff!
Part of my internet pottering was (hopefully) helping Francesca out. Her boy just turned five, which means her benefits take a kick to the balls, but being as I was just looking into this stuff a couple of weeks ago, I had a few links to recent memory that I could dig up and fire her way. She seemed pretty stoked, so hopefully it’ll help. Fairly sure she’ll manage to work something out anyway, either via my links or under her own steam, she seems pretty clever. In another time/place/multiverse I’d jump the fuck out of her bones.
Then kinda got sucked into a bit of a whirlpool of website checking, between disgracebook, macstories, daring fireball, Pat et all. Felt compelled to “catch up” on the stuff I’d missed last week or so. *But* that does kinda prove that I don’t need to spaff an hour or more on it every day. Like I think I spent *maybe* two hours on it (bar a couple of articles that got instapapered) which, once or twice a week has to be a little better than an hour or more a day? So that might be my new *thing*, rather than checking every day, just factor in some time in the week where that’s what I do. Just catch up on the “news”. So minimal internet use tomorrow, methinks, unless it’s actual focused use with some sort of goddamn *purpose*.
Played a little bit of bass too, probably about 20 minutes just dicking around and playing/singing along to stuff. Some songs are harder to do that too than others. *20 Flight Rock*, for example, has slightly weird timing that makes it not really work, but I’m sure with a bit of practice it’ll get better, and wasn’t too terrible anyway. *Johnny B Goode* though, something about that just doesn’t work for me. Playing is fine, it’s a straight up 12 bar, but either my voice doesn’t work for it, or it’s in the wrong key for me (as I was playing it in G, easily rectified) or I just need to get better (entirely possible).
Crap, I just cheated and had a look at the app store to see if the updates had finished. Bad, bad, Mike. While I’m cheating though, how much time have I got left? A little over 5 minutes. That isn’t too shabby, this feels like quite a few words. I wonder how I turn the word count on, sure it used to be there…….
Hmm, 762 words, but I have to finish the entry, then bring up the info pane. Seems a little convoluted. I guess writing in here isn’t really about the word count though, it’s just about getting the stuff out.
Oh man, the kids! Trav is being a proper shit, and I’m trying hard not to be horrible to him, but jeez he’s annoying sometimes! As soon as we got home, I remembered that we should do a plan so we knew what we were at tonight, so drew that up, something like:
- 30 minutes of chilling out
- Trav-20 minutes of project, Jelly help me put clothes away
- 10 minutes to decide on a movie to watch later
- Dinner
- Bath/shower/tidy up
- Movie
- Bed
Which seemed to work pretty well until the movie choosing part. They played a little mine craft (like 20 minutes) then we moved on, I started getting dinner sorted while they tried to pick a movie, the stipulation being that they both agreed on one. I took about 4 ttys at telling them not to forget that we had movies on the computer/internet so it didn’t *have* to be a DVD from the shelf before I got pissed off with it and made them stop and pay attention, but Trav kept wandering back to the DVD shelf so I called him up on it, warned him that he had to ***Listen*** to me when I was speaking to him or the choice wouldn’t be his any more, and then made it nearly all the way through my short “Don’t forget we have movies on the computer and internet” statement before he was off looking at something else again. Like it can’t be more than 20 second or so. So he failed on that one, and I think that just put him in a shitty mood for the rest of the night. Picking at soup, getting proper shirty with Jelly, generally just being a douche. Managed to get them both cleaned up though, and more washing done, and the kitchen aint too bad (though I’m gonna go sort it out properly after this). 
Tried to get a nap in (with a little help from *pzizz*) once they were in bed, but Jelly kept bugging me and it wasn’t that successful and took a bit longer than I’d planned, so I’m a bit behind schedule now. More so as the buzzer went off a few minutes ago. 
Pretty impressed with my word output though. Apart from that minor lapse, I’ve sat here and written for a good amount of time, and put out a good amount of words too. Hopefully a bit more practice at this will gelp/enable me to pump out a decent amount of words about an actual subject at some point, instead of just recounting my day. Tis a start though, learning the craft and all that.
Think I’ll do the kitchen now, grab a quick shower, and then tonight and tomorrow I shall mainly focus on finding actual work.

	Date:	19 November 2013 at 18:45:27 GMT
	Weather:	2°C Clear
	Location:	5–13 Station Road, Portishead, England, United Kingdom

Work in progress return to the blog type post. Getting there now I think. There's no real arc to it yet, and it's a bit all over the place (possibly due to having mostly been written on two entirely separate occasions a couple of weeks apart)


I've been slacker than usual at updating this site lately. Some if it could be blamed on moving across the country, or starting/joining a [new band](http://doctorcaligari.co.uk), or redesigning/moving this site while rewriting a lot of the "static" pages, or some other personal stuff that's been going on, but a lot of it has to do with my brain trying to work out what it is I'm meant to be doing. Both to get some cash rolling in, and a more basic (yea right!) "what is my contribution to this world"? I'm still not entirely sure, but in the process of trying to work that out and reading a lot to try and help me, I think I've learned to be ok with the not knowing. Slightly excited even to see where this train goes. [Everything is temporary anyway.](http://m.youtube.com/watch?v=a_saUN4j7Gw&desktop_uri=%2Fwatch%3Fv%3Da_saUN4j7Gw).
More music is up there for sure, and with a bit of  luck ([the harder you work, the luckier you will get](http://vimeo.com/42372767)) there'll be some interesting developments on that front shortly. I've also been thinking a lot about other areas where I have a modicum of skill or enjoyment, and how I can expand and improve on them. So expect more [photos](http://notacello.co.uk/photos) and stuff up soon. Initially there'll be a lot of older ones that I'll dig out of Aperture, but I shall be trying to get a proper camera out more, and/or think about what I'm doing when I take photos, and especially when I look back at the ones I've taken. As I've been trawling Aperture [[[insert screeny of photo count]]] I've realised that I'm a hoarder. There's a lot of plain *shit* pictures that I've shared, and a lot more that no one (including me) is ever going to want to look at, so why are they taking up so much of my precious drive space?
Pretty much though, I've come to the conclusion that what I really need to do is just work. At whatever, but put some damn effort in.

Finally applied to youth centre job:

Good afternoon,
I'd like to apply for either of the Youth Support Worker roles you have advertised. Ideally the first post with more hours, but the second post would work too!
I've recently moved to the area, but prior to that I was a teaching assistant at a Banbury Academy, helping students across the secondary age range get as much as possible from school, and hopefully life in general. A big chunk of my time there was spent in the Ace Base, sort of satellite school with its own building just outside the main school grounds. It served a select group of students who weren't coping with mainstream school. Apart from taking care of their academic needs, we did a lot of work on life and vocational skills such as cooking, shopping, setting a budget, healthy eating and the like. I'm hoping that, along with general life experience will translate well into working for you.  
I have a pretty huge interest in music, and dabble in other forms of art, and having two young children means the paint, glue, etc comes out quite often.

I've attached my CV, please let me know if there's anything else you'd like from me, I look forward to hearing from you soon!

	Date:	25 November 2013 at 09:03:24 GMT
	Weather:	3°C Mostly Cloudy
	Location:	72–78 West Hill, Portishead, England, United Kingdom

Today might be the day we break up 

	Date:	25 November 2013 at 10:07:18 GMT
	Weather:	4°C Mostly Cloudy
	Location:	89 West Hill, Bristol, England, United Kingdom

Ok, supposed to be writing to try and work out what’s in my head and try and save what’s left of my marriage. Very good at putting it off though, but I’ve got here eventually, which I guess counts for something. 
Was just looking at the shiny not so new *maps* feature in day one. Kinda cool seeing where I’ve written different stuff. Mostly though I’ve written from Banbury and now Portishead, with a few outliers. Worryingly, they mostly said the same sort of thing:
- “Must get better at x”
- “Really should tidy the house”
- “Want to write y”
There was even one from a bike ride saying “Wouldn’t be too bad if Emma didn’t want me to move out.” So it’s definitely an ongoing thing, even if I’ve only been capturing anything for a little over a year (August 2012 apparently, wish it’d been longer). 
No idea what to do. How to fix this.
Like I’m not sure I want this, but I don’t know that I’d be any better without it. That’s fear, I guess. Good ol’ mind-killer. So, how do I feel?
Pretty powerless, trapped. Like I can’t do *x* because of *y*, or until that other thing’s done, and that other thing is gonna cost money I don’t have, and I can’t do this thing to help make some money because of whatever. But how much of that is real, and how much of it is just my perception? Suddenly think all that crap I’ve read and listened to might be about to get applied to something. Just because it *feels* that way, doesn’t mean that it *is* that way. And now instead of pressing on I’m pissing around with formatting. Gotta be a disorder in there somewhere. Yay, go down another tangent! Or rabbit hole. Rat’s nest. Etc.
I’m not sure this is a good place to start, like I want to work out what’s up with me and Emma. But maybe it’s the first step on the path. Might get a lot of words done today.
Driving:
It’d be really good if I could drive. It’d make me a lot more versatile about where I could work (whether jobby job or something else) and also *when*. No more reliance on public transport. Think it’d contribute to me feeling better about myself too. More of an actual functioning human being. *BUT* until I get *a lot* of money rolling in, there’s no way I can pay for a license, or lessons, or tests, or even a car to drive with such lessons and tests. So for that example, it’s true. Trapped. A slight chicken/egg situation because having this thing that’s gonna cost a lot of money would help me make more money. But it’s not a crucial thing. I’ve managed to somehow make money for most of my life without having had a car, even if most of that wasn’t spent in quite such a back asswards little town as Portishead. Bristol is quite close though. But in *time* and convenience it’s kinda similar to the Banbury/Oxford relationship except there are less options for transport here, and (it seems so far) less opportunities for stuff to do here. There’s more going on in Banbury than there is in Portishead.
But there are things I could be doing to get some money coming in now. 
- Hit up some agencies
- Look for more jobs, even a crappy crap crap one
- *Finally* get around to selling the old laptops and junk that I’ve been carting around for years.
- *Start* doing some of the freelance stuff I’ve had ideas for. Fuck what everyone says/thinks. When transport becomes an issue, deal with it, or use whatever money I’ve made.
Made the mistake of going to the toilet, got roped into a “hug” for a while. Didn’t really have much to say, still trying to work it all out in here. Like my brain is not the place to work stuff out. I got so much more done with writing stuff down than I did with a lot more time just “trying to think”. 
And then another one, bit friendlier this time though. No, not *that* friendly, perv.
Driving isn’t the only thing where it feels like I could make some money if I spent some money. Getting a halfway decent amp setup would help immensely too I reckon. So I can turn up and look the part, at least. In the meantime, hopefully I’ll be able to get something worked out with that Behringer DI box and just be like “this is my setup, plug me into the desk and I shall sound awesome” and put off getting an amp too soon.


	Date:	25 November 2013 at 23:43:37 GMT
	Weather:	1°C Clear
	Location:	87–95 West Hill, Portishead, England, United Kingdom

I feel like if this is gonna work, we both need to be working on it, and pretty committed too. Otherwise I might as well just fuck off out the way and let her move in with mr "not quite a millionaire" and not have to worry about money and all that shit ever again.
Also working against me was my lack of a reaction to the revelation. A big part of that was down to me pretty much knowing already. Emma's sobbing "I thought we were over" a while back kinda clinched that for me, and to be honest, The relief that it wasn't anything worse probably cancelled a lot of the negativity too. The rest of it would just be me though. I don't get that worked up over things, in either direction :-/ Partly, too, I totally don't see how being a dick about anything helps at all, so I'm not about to start screaming or hitting or stomping off. I guess I think too much.
She did threaten (?) that she's just gonna keep doing stupider and stupider things to try and get a reaction, and apparently that' snot her fault, it's the way she was brought up or something. So what's next?
Apparently the reason I stick around is laziness. Not sure how accurate that is. Probably a tiny bit, but not a lot by any stretch. She is actually amazing, and anyone would be a *lucky bastard* to wake up with what I get to wake up next to. ***Anyone.*** This just seems like the only thing in my life I've ever not sucked terribly at, and to fail now would be miserable. Things can and have been amazing, and totally can be again

	Date:	11 August 2014 at 23:51:23 BST
	Weather:	14°C Mostly Cloudy
	Location:	89 West Hill, Bristol, England, United Kingdom

Hey baby, I couldn't really sleep last night, stupid brain. I wrote some stuff. For you. Some of it is from an angry me who's hurting, and I apologise if he's not very nice. Some of it though is from a friend who loves you very very much. More than anything. So take a bit of time to hear what he says. It won't be easy, so after work might be easier. I'll understand if you need some time. Love you.

So I'm writing this while you're asleep. I assume you're asleep, you haven't  moved much or said anything in a while, so I guess so.
So much going around my brain. Part of me wonders if there's some way to have an aneurism by sheer force of will.
I've been way too nice about this whole thing from the start. The night I come home and you think I've done something stupid (maybe I should've, this'd all be easier now, right?) , I get home and he's here, I fucking suck it up and take a deep breath and keep being nice, and *talking*. I don't find something heavy or sharp and try and bash his brains in. Maybe part of that was self preservation, he'd probably rip my arms off.
As some sort of rough time line:
- I didn't stop you talking to him
- I just got pissed off when you did, hoping you'd get the hint and stop
- I told you it bugged me
- this May have happened a few times. But I kept being so fucking *nice*, I understood he was a *friend* and we all need those.
- I asked you not to talk to him any more
- this, too, may have happened more than once
- last time, I think I actually showed a little emotion and balls and explained how much it was fucking me up, you having anything to do with him, and even changed your number (as requested) but you couldn't keep away.

I guess what I should've done is flat out told you right from the start not to have anything to do with him ever fucking again. But no, good old nice guy again.

I'm sure there was a point along there somewhere that I asked you to choose, because this kinda half way thing was shit. Pretty sure it was early on too. I don't see how either of you can think that fucking around with a married woman for any amount of time can ever have a happy ending. We've seen the movies.

How about, if you leave me (which is looking increasingly likely), I text you every week or two, reminding you of happy times, or the things we did really well, or how much I miss you and will never get over you and how special you are, and see how happy you are a year later? I'd bet some combination of not very, or you having a different number, or me getting a pasting, or some sort of restraining order or some shit. Only an idiot would let something like that stand, right? I know that's harsh, but it's also how it seems to have been going since we moved here, maybe even before.

I'm sorry it takes me to the brink of losing you to seem like I'm trying. Sometimes there's not much trying, but it's nearly always there. I'm not sure you've tried. See above. Especially after the number change. If you wanted us to work out, it would've ended then, surely. **You** were the only one able to contact him then. This isn't him texting you and you replying. Fuck you for that, and fuck me for not questioning it at the time of the "I changed my mind, I don't want a new number" text. Pretty big warning sign right there! Nope, head down, fingers crossed, go go optimism!

Then we get to last week, and you're all geared up to go, and I kinda get my head around it and just about break down when I open the bedroom door and see your bags packed, but somehow keep it together. At least until I'm in the shower, then whatever shell I've rigged up just crumbles and I'm on the floor making noises I didn't know I was capable of. 
And then a bit of a rough evening.
When I get home I half expect your bags by the door, ready to roll. Did you hear me walking up? Do you know how long it took me to go from standing by the wall, thinking stupid shit like "this is the last night it's "our" house", to working up to actually walk in. And then we talk and cry and get into bed and cry some more and then our bodies have their own little chat and for a little while that night I think I actually went insane. But then I woke up in the morning, not insane, with tentative hopes slightly raised. They were getting slightly higher (mostly, imagine a slightly wavy graph, but one that's definitely going generally up) but now I'm all fucked again. Once again contemplating just sneaking out, never to be seen again. 

But I don't think that's me. Of course when things get shit, I'm going to try and make them better. Yes, I need to get better at not letting thm get shit, but also, I think I am. I'm talking to you more, even if it's not always face to face, and I'm still tryingto improve. I need you working with me though, if that's to continue.
There have been things out of your control, I get that. Not everything though. One of the things that apparently would make life better is more time with the kids. You've been in charge of the hours you work to a pretty large extent for months now. More than you probably ever have? At least in a long while. Take more advantage of that! Whatever else you do.
You need to choose me or him. For reals. Three is definitely a crowd, and no one is ever going to be happy in this one. If you can't move, I can. There are cheap rooms for rent on gumtree, and I can be pretty cheap to feed on my own. I'll support you and the kids as much as I *possibly*can, always.
Work out what it is you want. Actual concrete, tangible things as much as possible. Try and avoid vague bullshit like "better". What do these things actually look like as you imagine them? I'm not sure you really know, it's hard working them out. For about the millionth time, **GET THINGS OUT OF YOUR HEAD**. It will either make each individual thing or idea more real, or more stupid. Both of which help. A lot.
I love you.

	Date:	11 August 2014 at 23:51:23 BST
	Weather:	14°C Mostly Cloudy
	Location:	89 West Hill, Bristol, England, United Kingdom

You need to choose me or him. For reals. Three is definitely a crowd, and no one is ever going to be happy in this one. If you can't move, I can. There are cheap rooms for rent on gumtree, and I can be pretty cheap to feed on my own.

The click was too much though so mostly played with compression and gates and eq and mic placement, which helped a bit, but came to type conclusion (again) that the action on the big bass is just too high. Got the files out and fixed that now though. I think the E might still be a little high, but it's definitely better than it was by a long way.

	Date:	26 August 2014 at 22:19:43 BST
	Weather:	15°C Partly Cloudy
	Location:	11 Avon Way, Bristol, England, United Kingdom

Emma's on a 10-close tomorrow, so oughta get more recording done, stoked! And actual recording this time, less dicking around. Get the bass done to the click and then redo guitars and add some vocals in! Maybe even get more than one song done?

	Date:	27 August 2014 at 00:00:00 BST

So, this morning so far:
Totally busted Trav pretending to shower.
Shower goes off, "come on then dude, I need a wee"
"just a sec I'm getting my top on"
Silence.
Steps out, hair slightly damp (almost as if he's wet his hands and then rubbed them in his head)
There's a clean patch on the mirror, I assume where he's rubbed the steam off to check if he looks sufficiently "showered".
I cop a feel of his completely dry towel as he walks past and say
"You haven't actually been in the shower though, have you?"
"No"
I pee, he goes back in and turns the shower on, a few minutes later 
"Dad, there's no hot water"
So I explained how the shower doesn't use the same water as the other taps, and there blatantly is hot water or the bathroom wouldn't be all steamy from the water and electricity he just wasted. I've been in and turned the shower on properly now, fingers crossed! Great start to the morning eh!?

Ate a fuckload of bacon, bread, biscuits and a donut when we got back, which probably helped undo our hard work. Feels weird that I need to try and stock up on carbs *and* protein. That’s like “just eat loads of shit” to me. Weighed myself earlier too, came in at 1212, which I think is pretty normal for me. Splat thinks I’ve lost weight though. Probably just toned up a bit. I may have to buy some scales at some point. Maybe not though. Very low priority. 
Pixelmator for ipad looks damn sweet. 
As does Elite Dangerous. May have to weigh up my options there. I could throw together a cheap windows box that’d do that and other games a bunch of justice way before I could afford to get one of those retina iMacs. And that’d kinda defeat part of the purpose of me getting said iMac. Even though it’d almost definitely be whatever they bring out next year, rather than this years model. I’m pretty sure my next “computer gear” cycle will be a desktop and a new ipad. The new airs are disturbingly close to recent laptops in raw processing power, and there’s not much that I need to get done that requires both mobility and horsepower. Geez, the comparisons I saw were to macbook airs of a newer vintage than the pro I’m typing this on, so they might even beat this thing. Jeebus.
Been chatting with Emma a bit too, think we’re ok. Defy still in love. Chatting to her on FaceTime hurt, really wanted to kiss her and couldn’t.

	Date:	20 July 2015 at 15:09:47 BST

Combo of things today. Gonna try the “just keep typing” exercise, *and* try and work out what’s going in my head. *Feelings* and such. Oops. I stopped typing. Think I’m kinda torn between really wanting to get this business thing off the ground (I’ve been reading/watching/listening to a lot about it lately. Which means thinking about it) and being aware that it’ll cost money (short of) and time (summer holidays). Not sure if that second part is just me making excuses though. Probably. I can definitely start, and get things ready to proper ramp up in September. 
Got business card/logo ideas done the other day. They cost money to get made though, and I wanna get the website set up too. Money again. I *can* get the copy written for the site though, so that’ll all be ready to go off the olde bat. 
Not sure about this whole family thing either. Not sure I want it. I know I want to want it, but that’s no quite the same. Things are still weird with me and Emma. Not AS weird, but still not quite there. Awkward. Trying to please. Just a bit fake, I guess. Something something something. Just keep my hands moving. Was walking home from tesco/dog walk with Bean yesterday (Trav was sleeping at Kyrons) and thought how we’d get on ok, just the two of us. She was crying like a loony for Emma when it was bed time though. Not sure it was entirely serious though, there was a lot of laughing too, think it was a play. 
Been playing a lot of computer games the last couple of days, that *definitely* isn’t helping. Feel a bit useless and neglectful, probably because it’s true. Neglectful and not accomplishing anything else. Bad combination. Ooooh, I got a new gun on Destiny, amazing! There’s something to be remembered for. There are books I’d rather read and things I’d rather do than unlock achievements on computer games. Gonna stop now because I just remembered one of those things I want to do. Bass!

	Date:	20 July 2015 at 23:59:00 BST

Took *all* the dogs for a walk too. They so fat n lazy! one lap of the field and they were done. Out of breath pretty much since we got there!
Kinda torn between trying to work out what we can do to fix things, like the last few days were really nice. Or should we just try and move on, find something else to do?
Been invited over for curry din dins tomorrow :/ Probably oughta go.
Made really damn awesome burgers tonight. Tess and Ann shared too. good stuff. And wedges. Big fat burny wedges. Fried up peppers too. Burgers wrapped in bacon. Greasy!
Really kinda wanting an apple watch now. Things keep conspiring to make me want one more I think. 
Had a go at ordering one along with a new MBP (kids need a computer for homework now, for reals), earlier, trying to do the credit/repayment thing. Failed.
Think my new plan is to pay off credit cards ASAP, and then either use that to buy stuff, (with my limit that will have gone up due to all my paying off) or get it on an actual proper credit agreement thing if I can. That’d be better. Cheaper too.
Prob ready to roll for the summer, might be new watch by then, and new computers for sure. Still torn between a pimped 13 and the stock 15 though. 15 makes more sense, but is like £450 more, which is a watch and some change.

Moving on is the plan though. Moving on.

I did not a lot yesterday. Pesky internet. Some of it was useful I guess, plans are afoot for kids crimbo presents and I got some other bits sorted out. Not a lot though. The list was still like andrex. Strong and very, very long.
So had a quick look at omnifocus this morning, picked a few things, binned some others, packed my bag, and the deal is that I get to go home when they’re all done. Till then, I’m out. This could take some time.
Got haircut, and had a good old chat with Giggsy. Think it was what I needed, like some actual conversation. It was good. Was coerced without much effort into a trip to the pub for what ended up being a couple of pints. More chats. Still good.
Might have blagged a gig in July. Not sure yet. Will see.

	Date:	28 September 2015 at 21:05:06 BST

Tough conversations today. Still not sure  what I’m meant to be doing.

Oh that explains why it's such a pretty colour and so big. Not purposely going to wake at 4 tho. 
We are so far apart now. Is that easier for you? 
Can't see the moon. 

Relationship: never understood it. Didn't ever understand why you liked me, you just did. Didn't get how it worked but it was fun. Tried to keep it but kept breaking a bit and wasn't as much fun. Tried harder = feeling like it's all in a sieve and there will be nothing left. U = just out of reach, can't get you, don't want me to touch you. 
Relationship ends:  everything sucks. I want you to fix it, not happening. Waiting for you to rescue me, I know u can't. 
Onwards: want to get away from you. 10 hours a week you see the kids hurts, (spend a massive proportion of that time longing for you to do anything) 
Want to be with anyone else, want it all to stop. Can't because it feels like I'm betraying you, like I'm taking it passed retrievable. But isn't it already?

I can't do this again. There's  being nice and there's being taken for a fucking fool.
It's been the same since we moved here. Sure, see this guy, oh, he loves you, sure see him some more, invite him out for drinks, get him to take the family to the fucking zoo, why not? Kiss him even. That's ok, let's try and fix this. I've been nice, I've been trying to do what you want. Trying to keep you happy and the thanks I get is looking after the kids so you can go out with your new man. Not happening again.
Jesus I've been stupid.


	Date:	6 October 2015 at 23:59:00 BST

Maybe I’ll do something stupid like “So, if a guy wanted to take you out, what’d be the best way of asking?” Or something. How’d I get your number, I was going to say, but the other one might be better.

What is there to lose? I gain a slightly awkward feeling, and potentially some romance.
She says no, then what? Laugh it off as a nothing ventured, nothing gained thing. “Shit, sorry, thought I’d ask though. No worries.” A guy’s gotta try, right?

Well, today turned into a clusterfuck.
I don't know where I fit in any more. Even less so

	Date:	20 October 2015 at 18:50:20 BST
	Weather:	10°C Cloudy

So yesterday wasn’t too bad.
Today, kinda fucked up, didn’t get the things done I wanted to. Woke up a bit later, spent ages futzing with the printer, then realised it had no ink anyway, so a total blowout. Could’ve gone to the freelancers thing but bottled it. Could’ve emailed my CV, but didn’t do that either. Guess I’ll email it tomorrow AM, then pop in for waffles and see if the lady is around. Ideally by 11ish so they’re not too busy yet. Not sure on their timings.
Then kinda not looking forward to seeing the kids. No, seeing the kids ok, seeing Emma not so much. Haul arse, get stuck with Lewis waiting for, then on, the bus, pick the kids up, then Jelly gets jumped by Emma, and “Chris is in the house” and “My phone is in his car”. 
I don’t even wanna know. Just ew.
So angry. Somehow it’s probably my fault, heaven forbid I assume I’ll be doing the same thing I’ve done every Tuesday for a while. Fuck OFF.
Been very hard maintaining the “dad out with daughter” face and not letting out the “Pissed off ex husband” face. Might’ve slipped a couple of times, nothing too major though. 
I was all up for getting out of there and coming back later, but instead, I got to wait outside while Jelly and Lily bounced on the trampoline and Emma went and got her karate stuff. Not too awkward then.
Then a trip to macdonalds and a bunch of hanging around trying to kill time before karate. Still rocked up 20 minutes early.
Shame Sam or Bev isn’t here, I’d be more than happy to chuck Bean in the car with them to drop off.
Possibly childish. 
Really hoping I don’t get roped into going inside tonight.
Pretty sure it’d be impossible.

	Date:	20 October 2015 at 23:59:00 BST

Gone and all fucked myself up again. *again*.
Emma brought bean into work for some trick or treating practice. She was wearing *that* little red dress and looking fucking *smoking*.
Jeez, what a mistake I made.
What I'm trying to remember is that I do have some control over how I react to the things that happen in the world. They're just things that happen. I have to take some control back about my own reactions and how I choose to let myself feel about things.
Last night though, I was pretty wrecked. Like I didn't want to se her more, and it's easier to hate her for what she's doing, but I knew that if she'd asked, I would've been there in a heartbeat. Feeling bad about it and dirty and used but thoroughly indulging knee deep in the whole thing. Beyond knee deep.
Fortunately (?) she didn't.
Just the fear and loneliness to keep me company

	Date:	29 November 2015 at 14:54:59 GMT
	Weather:	11°C Overcast
	Location:	Clapton Lane, Bristol, England, United Kingdom

Jeez, what a couple of days.
Haircut Friday, good chats and cd swapping with Giggsy, then a downturn when going to see the kids, as in everyone was out, kinda lost it a little, popped into work for coffee and to get some laptop stuff done.
Had arranged to meet Sam for G's music thing at Venga, but that was cancelled because he was sick (bummer number two).
Sat around for a bit deciding on more drinks or more going home, drinks helped us decide that more drinks were the right idea.
Left impy, hit up Venga, and bumped into Rachel (Joes ex) and a couple of her mates, then on to the plough, where things start to get hazy. Barney was a right mess.
Then start getting grinded on by one of Rachel's mates, Becks. Known for the night as "Plaits". Looking back this is a lot clearer, but didn't take full notice at the time. Ended up heading back to impy where she jumped me and was definitely attached to my face pretty sturdily. Lots of grinding and touching ensued, magical night.
Then there was missing phone drama, phone calls, a reunion with Rachel and a saying goodbye.
So hanging yesterday, barely human.
Apparently she's seeing someone, so no future dates, yet.
Restored my faith a little though, like if it can happen once it can happen again, even if it's not with her. Oh, high/lowlight, she's 18!

	Date:	30 November 2015 at 00:34:00 GMT

	Date:	2 December 2015 at 14:27:47 GMT
	Weather:	12°C Mostly Cloudy
	Location:	Serbert Way, Bristol, England, United Kingdom

Recap of Friday for Reike, as requested:

We started off drinking White Russians (vodka, kahlua, and milk/cream. V tasty!) at Impero. Discussing whether we should go home and save cash (because G was too sick for music), then had some food there and a few beers. I think the beers helped us decide that we should have some more beers!
Then we went to Venga and had some rum. All the rum.
Met up with a few more people, had more rum, for some reason we FaceTime called one of the ladies parents....
There were lots of nicknames. Some of the people we knew already, some we just met. Sleepy, plaits, hair, beardy, too short, I forget the others.
Barney (not sure if you met him, little scruffy guy, might've been at the plough when we played shithead) was pretty damn drunk by this point, and was kinda like a chimpanzee. Walking funny, and every now and then he'd just grab your hand and start stroking it.
Eventually Venga closed so we ended up wandering down to the plough. Met more people we knew (this is where it gets hazy, some of them I'd forgotten seeing that night until I've seen them again since), had more beers, got pretty rowdy.
Then the next thing I remember is being back at Impero with one of the girls we met in Venga, which is probably the good/bad thing I said about earlier. Bad Mike!
Slept there, and then it's 08:10 and Chef is knocking on the door to get let in to work, I was meant to start at 8!
Started off feeling ok on Saturday, but was feeling pretty dead by the time I finished work. Went home, was in bed by 7, got more sleep, then working again all day Sunday. 

Think that's most of it!


	Date:	2 December 2015 at 15:07:49 GMT
	Weather:	12°C Mostly Cloudy
	Location:	26 Down Road, Bristol, England, United Kingdom

Lots of waking up and weird dreams last night.

 Something's bothering me... Why did u choose to spend the time with the kiddos to fix comp? 

Because Travis asked me to. Didn’t take long. Time it. Unplug everything one at a time and plug it back in, flick the switch on the back, hit the power button, flick the switch at the back, hit the power button, turn it off at the wall, pick it up, rest it sideways on your legs, open the side up, gently jiggle some wires, put the door back on, hit the button, et voilà. Add on a couple of minutes for poking the cables round the back of the desk. Literally he walked in, “Hello Poppy, hi Dad, can you fix the red computer for me please? The power doesn’t work.”
I wasn’t exactly keen to go in that room, if that’s what you’re worried about

Looks like it could be a pretty good week. Apart from docs/sad class on thursday (and the need to book an actual doc appointment) I’ve got all the day times to myself to hopefully get stuff done.

> polymath (n.)
> "person of various learning," 1620s, from Greek polymathēs "having learned much, knowing much," from polys "much" (from PIE root \*pele- (1) "to fill") \+ root of manthanein "to learn" (from PIE root \*mendh- "to learn"). Related: Polymathy "acquaintance with many branches of learning" (1640s, from Greek polymathia "much learning"); polymathic.

	Date:	22 June 2021 at 10:31:07 BST
	Weather:	14°C Mostly Cloudy
	Location:	40 Kildare Road, Bristol, England, United Kingdom

OK. So I’ve royally fucked up almost everything. This’ll be a big old attempt to get “feelings” down, and some of the bullshit in my head *out* of my head, in some attempt to deal with them, and potentially show to some future therapist or something so they can work out what’s broken in me and help fix it. It’s not going to be easy, and will in fact ​*suck royally,*​ but it’ll eventually help. 
And that’s a pretty decent amount of procrastination already. Writing about the writing instead of doing the actual writing. 
I am, can be, really selfish. Something something duality of man because apparently I’m also really nice/helpful. I was doing some thinking last night, on people I have in some way fucked over, with varying degrees of intentionality, although mostly through apathy really. This goes back a looooong way. Entirely to those closest to me, pretty much. Could be that’s why I (sometimes?) try to keep some sort of distance? Or I realise I’m bound to fuck up, so after it’s too late and they’re close/attached, I then try to distance myself, maybe to minimise pain/harm/something, and that just makes things worse, speeds up the end of that relationship, and I tend to just shrug and let it happen. Again, because I’m trying to minimise pain I guess, and in my head I’m trying to do what’s best for them, like “this is what they want (to break up), what I want is less important than that, so away we go”. 
There’s definitely been a pattern. The conversations I’ve had with Shiv clearly echo ones I had with Emma (in my 20’s, 30’s), and reflecting further back, even with Becky (LATE teens), and friends like Ray. Thinking about it, even potential conquests, like Kate, Kayleigh, Sarah, etc, if I’d taken more of a “this is what I want” approach, instead of going with some idea of what I thought it was they wanted, things could’ve turned out a lot differently. Stupid fucking low profile “I’m here if you want, just let me know” bullshit when I suck at taking hints (insert list of people having to explicitly tell me someone fancies me here). I think in all my relationships I’ve taken a bit of a passive stance. Like “Oh, this person shows an interest, cool” and then just seeing where it goes. I do feel like I’ve almost always done what I could (and do what I can) to guess what they’re after, and try and do things to make them happy, regardless of whatever it is I *really* want. At the same time though, I’ll totally do some really selfish/hurtful things (fuck work, I’m going to college now, have fun supporting everything/this is *my* money, I’m buying cigarettes or whatever, etc). Something something empathy? Am I actually depressed or just a fucking asshole? I guess that’s the scariest question of starting this whole process. “There’s nothing medically wrong, you’re just a bit of a dick, have fun with that”. I guess that’d at least be progress of a kind. Then again, I’m aware that humans generally shouldn’t feel this way, and I don’t have to. 
I think something might’ve just clicked into place. Like eventually I end up thinking that people/situations/whatever would be better off without me. Like 99.9999% of the time, and that could go for almost anything. So I get that particular demon dog on my heels, and slip into apathy. Like “yep, better off without me, I should just go die/live in the woods/busk my way across Europe/make it look like an accident” and while I’m dwelling on that/conjuring up fantasies of my inglorious exit, my effort levels drop through the floor. Minimal would be an overstatement. Understatement? Whatever. What I guess I should be doing instead is maybe looking at what’s broken, or why I feel bad about it, or why I’ve stopped trying, and attempt to do the opposite. It’s not going to be easy though, there’s a fucking lifetime of this cycle to break out of. I guess it’s self perpetuating too though. Like feel shit->do nothing->feel shit for doing nothing->do more nothing->get called out for being shit->feel shit->do nothing. That would make a good diagram. 
Not really sure what else to write, I’m gonna stop there for now, come back to it at some point. Peace out.
