	Date:	25 January 2022 at 16:20:39 GMT
	Weather:	4°C Clear
	Location:	38 Kildare Road, Bristol, England, United Kingdom

# Starting to feel generally a bit better I think. 
New keyboard is pretty rad. Discovered yesterday that I’ve got pretty much two weeks worth of holiday to burn through before the end of March, which is annoying, and gonna be hard. On the bright side, looks like I’ll get a month or two of shorter hours, but still end up with the same pay. 
My plan is to use that to get more creative stuff done. Like a website again. I’ve been playing around with *Obsidian* a whole bunch, and they’re kinda inbuilt publishing platform looks pretty cool. Before I pull the trigger though, I want to port most of what was on the old site over, but get it all talking to each other through mostly backlinks rather than tags. Bit of a monumental task, especially as I’ll be integrating it into a huge chunk of all my notes ever taken on anything, but once that’s going, it should be pretty cool. Adding to it will just be writing in the app I’m using for that anyway, and hitting a publish button when it’s ready to go. 
The only other slight hurdle is the way it handles photos, as I wanna get a lot of them up there, and redone. I’ve been through a few, and the editing choices I’d make now are different from the ones I did back then, both in terms of actual image manipulation, and which ones would make the cut to being published/shared. There are some that are almost like fun pics that I enjoy, but that aren’t particularly a good example of the craft, and if I’m putting stuff out, I want it to be like the crem de la crem or whatever. Maybe the best stuff only lives on the site, but gets mixed in with some of the not so good on instas/etc. 
Should probably have a chat with Shiv about plans too. It’s hard to get anything interesting done when any time spent not watching the same stuff over again seems like it gets me in trouble. 
Like today is a day off work for me, and I’ve done a few loads of laundry, un/loaded the dishwasher and fired that up, made breakfast smoothies, patched up a fence sorta, fed the dogs, made coffee, emptied bins, cut my hair, had a shower, and earlier I got shouted at for spending time playing a game on my phone. And I’m like *this* close to just firing back with a “well what did *you* do last time you had a day off?” Because it was, as very often the case, fuck all. 
So yea, wanna get more stuff done. Write more, take more photos, get a website running again to showcase this stuff, and get some more shirts or something knocked up. Produce. Getting paid would be a pretty big bonus, it’d be nice to transition away from working for someone else too. Actually, fuck that, with the part time gig going away, I need more income yesterday, or I very quickly will be barely making rent, let alone being able to do anything else. 
For now though, I must go, so as to prepare something for dinner, and allow the devices to update to 15.3 n stuff. 
But boy, I really dig this keyboard. That could be another problem, now I want some sort of contraption for this to work better with the ipad. Like another stand/case or something. Ho hum, first world problems. 
But writing more would be good I think. There’s been more than a few times recently where I’ve been reading something, or (more often) listening to a podcast, and just shaking my head, hopefully not in an arrogant way, but just a “I know this stuff better than these guys that are getting paid for it” way. And I think if it’s spun right, the voice of the “normal” person, who doesn’t necessarily *rely* on this stuff for work (yet), or do this for a living, could be valuable/needed on the scene. But only if done well. Like I need the professional quality, but without the point of view of someone who’s entire life pretty much revolves around tech and this ecosystem. I think the combo of that viewpoint, and the ease (once it’s set up) of using Obsidian to do it, will be valuable. Just gotta get it done. At the least, it might help me stumble into doing stuff for someone else that I’ll find more rewarding, and will build my chops. Cos jeez, getting a gig at MacStories or similar would be some next level shit, and I’ve got all the gear/setup for podcasting if that was to happen too. Would need a new desk/workspace though. But I think I need that anyway, getting anywhere near the way of Shiv when they’re working is not a pleasant experience, for anyone involved. 

And that’s nearly 1000 words bashed out in nearly an hour. Chuck in some editing time and that’s a decent couple of posts a week. Maybe I could “go live” sooner than I’d been thinking. Old photos can be added as I get round to it. Chuck a few up nowish, and work through the backlog. Fuck it, same with posts really, Start a bit more barebones/not linked, and work through the whole fucking thing in public. YES. Now that’s got me a bit excited, and I was typing a little harder than strictly required. 
Woo hah. Smoko, then dins, Like I said ages ago.

	Date:	26 January 2022 at 20:34:17 GMT
	Weather:	5°C Partly Cloudy
	Location:	40 Kildare Road, Bristol, England, United Kingdom

Pretty glad I got some stuff written this morning, I can kinda see what Merlin as on about on the last [ROTL](https://overcast.fm/+BmENaaVwo)
A bit of writing in the morning can help you feel less crappy about life in general. 
Not had a particularly outstanding day at work, deffo not the most productive, and think I kinda narced John a bit with not really caring that Jess started a bit late, and I forgot to get the pesky fucking coffee machine sorted out before I left. Standard cleaning cycle loop. 
Part of me is pretty tempted to move *everything* over into obsidian, it really is like it’s own little operating system going on over there. BUT, I also fully see the advantages of keeping things separated. Like journal in here, to-do’s in Omnifocus, etc etc. Then again, there is the option of having multiple vaults. So one as the blog, one as the journal, one for tasks. Sounds like a ball ache and a bunch of extra friction though, because I’d always have to be making sure I was in the right one, and switching between them, and it’d take away part of the immediacy/speed/simplicity of “I want to do this thing now, so I click on this”. So almost definitely a bust for task management, but a possibility for the journaling I suppose. “Writing goes here”. I think that could be one of the advantages of obsidian. Stuff goes in, you do a little admin work on tags n back links n stuff, et voila, All the everything. I bet it’s pretty simple to set the publish up to ignore anything tagged “journal” or whatever too, so *potentially* I could import everything from here (Day One, potential future self), and have that live in obsidian too. Perhaps. Might do a little research, see if anyone else has made a similar transfer.

	Date:	17 March 2022 at 14:26:57 GMT
	Weather:	12°C Mostly Cloudy
	Location:	38 Kildare Road, Bristol, England, United Kingdom

Might be the end of the line for day one. Was listening to the *focused* podcast yesterday, and discovered that there’s a tasks plug-in for obsidian! 
Sounds like it could be right up my fiddly ADD brain’s street. As I’m writing something/anything, it looks like I can just drop in a #task (might change this to #todo), et voila, it’s a thing. Then through the tasks and dataview plugins, I can just see them. So I can leave tasks dotted throughout my writing, but easily get a view of just them, but also quickly get to them in context, for reminders about where my brain was when I came up with the idea in the first place. Right now I’m waiting on Shiv to finish a call before I sneak upstairs and get a system rigged up. Between that and daily notes, if I’m kinda journaling in there, and can simply tag stuff to turn it into a task (then deal with contexts and things later) this could very well become ​*the way*​.
Got a couple of hours to crank away on it before feeding time hits again. Not 100% confident on having all my eggs is one basket, but the advantages seem pretty amazing. Things can go from random pre-coffee journaling thought to published article (or work in progress) within the same app/workflow/ecosystem. More (quantity) of notes will just feed the system too, growing the links and graphs and stuff and potentially spawning even more ideas which’ll hopefully turn into more output and something something virtuous cycle. Fingers crossed emoji.
In other news, finally tested negative today, and do feel back to about 90% I think. Noticed a good ol dent in the wall by the bathroom from when I keeled over though :-/
No NMS yet today, *hopefully* gonna get on there for a bit with Splat this evening though. Might have to try and claim that even if he’s out though.
And probably back to the day job tomorrow, joy to the world I guess. I wonder how much trouble I’m in? Oh yea, need to get a sick note sorted out, the service was down when I had a go yesterday
